text:
## Headline

Nimbus management used its earnings call to reiterate fiscal 2026 revenue guidance, raise the operating-margin outlook, and detail integration plans for the pending Cirrus Analytics acquisition.

## Q&A signals

- On Cirrus integration timing, Daniel Cho said "we expect native Cirrus query capabilities to be generally available inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026" [Q1].
- On the margin guide, Helena Vargas attributed "about 60 basis points to the hiring slowdown" and "about 40 basis points to underlying gross-margin improvement in Stratus" [Q2].
- On competition, Daniel Cho noted "our win rate against the hyperscaler-native offerings remained above 75 percent in Q2" [Q7].

## Commitments

- Nimbus expects to "close the previously announced Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026, which will materially expand our footprint in real-time log analytics." [K1]
- Management is "reiterating our previously communicated full-year fiscal 2026 revenue guidance of 2.55 to 2.60 billion dollars" [K2].
- Management said "we now expect full-year non-GAAP operating margin to land at approximately 19 percent, up from our prior guidance of 18 percent." [K3]
- Management said Nimbus expects "to deliver positive GAAP operating income for the full fiscal year 2026, which would be a first for Nimbus." [K4]
- On product integration, "we expect native Cirrus query capabilities to be generally available inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026." [K5]
- On cross-sell, "our early modelling points to roughly 40 million dollars of incremental ARR from existing Stratus customers adopting Cirrus during fiscal 2027" [K6].
